catalog-project-step-1-base-class.title: Step 1: Build the base catalog item
catalog-project-step-1-base-class.prompt: Create `CatalogItem` with shared title state and a basic label method.
catalog-project-step-1-base-class.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: Build a catalog that uses inheritance, overriding, abstraction, and polymorphism across files.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

catalog-project-step-1-base-class.starterCode:
from models.catalog_item import CatalogItem

item = CatalogItem("Starter")
print(item.label())

catalog-project-step-4-polymorphic-servi.title: Step 4: Add a polymorphic catalog service
catalog-project-step-4-polymorphic-servi.prompt: Create a service that receives mixed catalog items and calls `label()` on each.
catalog-project-step-4-polymorphic-servi.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: Build a catalog that uses inheritance, overriding, abstraction, and polymorphism across files.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

catalog-project-step-4-polymorphic-servi.starterCode:
from models.book_item import BookItem
from models.video_item import VideoItem

items = [BookItem("Python", "Ana"), VideoItem("OOP", 9)]
for item in items:
    print(item.label())

help.concept: Build a catalog that uses inheritance, overriding, abstraction, and polymorphism across files.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

catalog-project-step-5-abstract-fee.starterCode:
from models.book_item import BookItem
from models.video_item import VideoItem
from services.catalog import catalog_lines

items = [BookItem("Python", "Ana"), VideoItem("OOP", 9)]
for line in catalog_lines(items):
    print(line)

catalog-project-step-6-load-final-catalo.title: Step 6: Load and summarize the final catalog
catalog-project-step-6-load-final-catalo.prompt: Load mixed item rows from a data file and produce the final polymorphic summary.
catalog-project-step-6-load-final-catalo.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: Build a catalog that uses inheritance, overriding, abstraction, and polymorphism across files.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

catalog-project-step-6-load-final-catalo.starterCode:
from models.book_item import BookItem
from models.video_item import VideoItem
from services.catalog import catalog_lines, total_fees

items = [BookItem("Python", "Ana", 2), VideoItem("OOP", 9, 1)]
for line in catalog_lines(items):
    print(line)
print(f"Fees: ${total_fees(items)}")

sc-module-9-catalog-polymorphism-project.title: Check the design choice
sc-module-9-catalog-polymorphism-project.prompt: Why does this module 9 catalog polymorphism project require each step to keep all files, not only the file changed in that step?
sc-module-9-catalog-polymorphism-project.hint: Choose the option that keeps object responsibilities clear.

help.concept: Cumulative project steps must preserve the whole runnable app state.
help.hint_1: Look for the answer that reduces duplication without hiding behavior.
help.hint_2: A good OOP answer usually keeps behavior near the object that owns the data.

options.b: Because the next step starts from the full previous project state and must still run.
options.c: Because only README files are graded.
options.d: Because imports work even when files are missing.

project-story.title: Module 9 Catalog Polymorphism Project: project story
project-story.bodyMarkdown: This graded project is cumulative. Each step starts from the exact full solution of the previous step, so the project grows like a real multi-file app.
